name: Visual Effects & Polish

criteriaResults.description: Camera.shake method exists with configurable intensity and duration
criteriaResults.evidence: Camera.shake is a function. Camera.shakeX and Camera.shakeY properties exist. Calling Camera.shake(10, 0.5) then updateShake() produces non-zero offsets (shakeX=-7.03, shakeY=-8.71) that decay over the duration.

criteriaResults.description: Screen shake applied to render pass
criteriaResults.evidence: states.js _renderStage() applies ctx.translate(Camera.shakeX, Camera.shakeY) when shake is active. ctx.save()/ctx.restore() ensures HUD is not affected. All game rendering (parallax, tiles, enemies, player, particles) is offset.
enemies.js _damageEnemy() calls Camera.shake(6, 0.3) when boss is hit — matches design spec (6px, 300ms). Line 2744.
player.js takeDamage() calls Camera.shake(4, 0.2) — matches design spec (4px, 200ms). Line 888.

criteriaResults.description: Player sprite hit flash on damage
criteriaResults.evidence: Player.hitFlash set to 4 frames in takeDamage() (line 885). Renderer checks hitFlash > 0 and overlays white (#FFFFFF) at 0.85 alpha (renderer.js:1322-1327). hitFlash overrides invincibility blink (line 1262).

criteriaResults.description: Enemy and boss hitFlash on damage
criteriaResults.evidence: All enemy render functions check hitFlash (e.g., e.hitFlash > 0 && e.hitFlash % 2 === 0 triggers '#FFFFFF' fill). Found in all enemy types: Shroomba, Sandcrawler, Frostwing, Lavaslime, Citadel enemies, and all 5 bosses. hitFlash initialized to 0 in spawn().

Renderer._initLeafParticles creates 30 leaf particles with forest colors (COLORS.forest.leaf, deepCanopy, highlight, bark, #6BC060). updateAndRenderLeaves() gates on world === 0 (Forest). Particles drift downward with sinusoidal horizontal sway via wobble.

Renderer._initSandParticles creates 35 sand particles with desert colors. updateAndRenderSand() gates on world === 1 (Desert). Particles drift primarily horizontally (speedX 0.5-1.7) with slight downward (speedY 0.05-0.2).
Renderer._initSnowParticles creates 80 snow particles using COLORS.tundra.snowWhite. updateAndRenderSnow() gates on world === 2 (Tundra). Snow drifts down with slight horizontal wobble.

Renderer._initEmberParticles creates 60 ember particles using COLORS.volcano.lavaOrange, moltenYellow, darkRed. updateAndRenderEmbers() gates on world === 3 (Volcano). Embers rise upward (negative speedY) with wobble and pulsing alpha.

criteriaResults.description: World-specific particles gated by world ID
criteriaResults.evidence: Each system checks _getCurrentWorld() and returns early if not matching: Leaves (world !== 0), Sand (world !== 1), Snow (world !== 2), Embers (world !== 3). Each also resets its _initialized flag on world change.

criteriaResults.description: Player death particles (4+ pieces with gravity)
criteriaResults.evidence: Particles.spawnDeathParticles spawns 9 rectangular particles with gravity=0.15. Runtime test confirmed: 9 particles spawned, all with gravity > 0 and shape='rect'. Particles burst radially outward.

criteriaResults.description: Death particle colors match player palette
criteriaResults.evidence: Death colors: [COLORS.playerBlue (#3A7BD5), COLORS.playerBlueDark (#2A5BA5), COLORS.playerSkin (#E8DCC8), '#3A3A4A']. 4 unique colors confirmed at runtime. Matches player sprite palette (blue body, skin tone, dark accent).
enemies.js _onBossDefeated() sets Game.timeScale = 0.3 (within 0.2-0.4 range). setTimeout restores to 1.0 after 1000ms (within 700-1500ms range). Line 2370-2373.
Boss defeat spawns 30 particles in a radial burst pattern (loop i < 30). Particles have 1.5s lifetime, mix of rect/circle shapes, gravity=0.08.

World-specific color palettes: Forest (leaf, deepCanopy, highlight, bark), Desert (sand, darkSand, lightStone, bleachedBone), Tundra (iceBlue, snowWhite, deepIce, auroraGreen), Volcano (lavaOrange, moltenYellow, darkRed), Citadel (multi-color including #FF69B4). Lines 2328-2343.

criteriaResults.description: Boss defeat shake stronger than hit
criteriaResults.evidence: Boss defeat: Camera.shake(12, 0.5). Boss hit: Camera.shake(6, 0.3). Ratio: 2.0x intensity (meets >=2x requirement). Duration also longer (0.5s vs 0.3s).
Transition.start() checks GameState.current === STAGE to center on Player position. Falls back to CANVAS_WIDTH/2, CANVAS_HEIGHT/2 for menus. Re-centers on opening phase for new scene.
Transition.duration = 0.25s per phase. Has hold phase (1 frame). Easing: close uses t*t (quadratic ease-in), open uses 1-(1-t)*(1-t) (quadratic ease-out). Total ~500ms.

Particles.spawnLandingDust spawns 5 tan particles. Called in Player._updateGroundMovement() (line 291) when onGround && !_wasOnGround && _prevVy > 2. Also called in wall slide landing (line 401) and crouch slide landing (lines 795, 800).
Particles.spawnWallSlideSparks spawns 3 white/yellow sparks. Called in Player._updateWallSlide() every 3 frames (line 462). Sparks spray from wall contact point using wallDir.
Particles.spawnRunDust spawns 2 particles per call. Called in Player._updateGroundMovement() every 6 frames when running (line 312). Particles trail behind player based on facing direction.

Game.timeScale exists, defaults to 1.0. Game loop uses scaledTimestep = FIXED_TIMESTEP / timeScale (game.js:236). Values < 1.0 increase the scaled timestep, reducing effective update frequency for slow-motion.
Particles.spawnBlockBreak spawns 8 debris particles (confirmed at runtime). Particles use block-colored debris colors, rect shapes with gravity=0.12.
Game runs at 60 FPS consistently. After 5+ seconds of runtime: fps=60, frameCount=304. No frame drops observed during automated testing.
Zero JavaScript console errors during full game load and 5+ seconds of runtime. No pageerror events fired.
Canvas visible (960x540), debug API available, Game.init() completes. All script files load correctly. Title screen renders.

Linear decay: intensity reduces proportionally to remaining time (progress = shakeTimer / shakeDuration). Early frames max offset ~15.64, late frames max offset ~1.33. Final values reach exactly (0, 0) when timer expires.

regressionResults.details: All 30 regression checks passed. Core engine (canvas 960x540, TILE_SIZE=32, gravity, input), player mechanics (update, physics, wall jump, coyote time), state machine (GameState, Transition, HUD, Menu), world map & save (WorldMap, SaveSystem, Collectibles), enemies & bosses (spawn, spawnBoss, _onBossDefeated), world palettes (desert, tundra, volcano), audio (AudioManager), particles, camera shake — all verified functional. Game runs at 60 FPS with timeScale=1.0.

designSpecCompliance.screenShake: Matches design spec: player hurt 4px/200ms, boss hit 6px/300ms, explosion 8px+(12px)/400ms+(500ms). Linear decay as specified.
designSpecCompliance.particles: Landing dust (5 particles, tan), run dust (2 per footstep), wall sparks (3, white/yellow), death (9 rect particles, player palette), boss defeat (30 particles, radial burst) — all match design spec quantities and behaviors.
designSpecCompliance.irisWipe: 500ms total (250ms close + hold + 250ms open). Quadratic ease-in for close, ease-out for open. Player-centered in gameplay, canvas-centered in menus. Matches design spec exactly.
designSpecCompliance.ambientParticles: Forest leaves (30, drift down with sway), Desert sand (35, horizontal drift), Tundra snow (80, gentle fall), Volcano embers (60, rise with glow) — all world-gated, exceed 20 minimum.
designSpecCompliance.colorPalette: All new VFX use design spec colors: player blue #3A7BD5, skin #E8DCC8, world palettes for boss defeat particles. Hazard red #FF4444 used in volcano defeat.

designQuality.notes: Visual effects system is well-integrated with the existing design language. Screen shake uses design-spec values (4/6/8px). Ambient particles use world palette colors exclusively. HUD is correctly excluded from shake effects via save/restore. Death animation uses player sprite colors. Iris-wipe with easing curves provides smooth, professional transitions.

originality.notes: World-specific ambient particles are a nice touch — leaves, sand, snow, embers each behave differently (drift directions, wobble patterns). Boss defeat combines slow-motion + explosion + screen shake for dramatic effect. Per-world boss defeat particle colors add thematic cohesion. The Citadel multi-color celebration is a thoughtful detail.

codeQuality.notes: Clean separation of concerns: Camera handles shake state, Particles handles spawning, Renderer handles world-specific ambient effects, enemies.js handles boss defeat choreography. Linear shake decay is simple and effective. World gating pattern (check _getCurrentWorld, return early) is consistent across all 4 ambient systems. timeScale integration into the fixed-timestep loop is minimal and correct.

uxPolish.notes: All VFX serve gameplay feedback: hit flash confirms damage, screen shake conveys impact, death particles show destruction. Boss defeat slow-mo creates a cinematic moment. Ambient particles add atmospheric depth without distracting. No frame drops — effects are performant. HUD unaffected by screen shake is important UX detail.

completeness.notes: All 28 criteria satisfied. Screen shake system complete with decay. Hit flash on both player and all enemy/boss types. All 4 worlds have ambient particles plus proper gating. Death animation with player-colored pieces. Boss defeat with slow-mo, world-colored particles, strong shake. Iris-wipe fully specified. Dust/spark particles all functional. Time scale integration working.

summary: Sprint 11 (Visual Effects & Polish) passes all 28 criteria and all 30 regression checks. The implementation adds a comprehensive visual effects layer: screen shake (4 trigger points with design-spec intensities), hit flash (player + all enemies/bosses), world-specific ambient particles (4 distinct systems, properly gated), death animation (9 particles in player colors), boss defeat choreography (slow-mo + 30-particle world-colored explosion + strong shake), and iris-wipe transitions (250ms phases with easing). All effects integrate cleanly with existing systems at 60 FPS.
